question: How do I contact Axon or what is Axon's address?
plain_english_answer: Axon supports business clients in Singapore, the Philippines and remotely worldwide. For fastest help, use WhatsApp or the contact form. Do not assume a walk-in office visit unless Axon confirms an appointment. If you need a meeting, share your company name, website, issue and preferred time.
recommended_action: Use WhatsApp Axon or the Contact page and include your website URL, email domain, screenshots or issue summary.
when_to_call_axon: Contact Axon when the matter involves your website, hosting, email, cloud, AI, automation, security, payment gateway or a business technology decision.
follow_up_questions: What company are you contacting from?
What website or domain is involved?
Is this urgent or for consultation?
cta_text: Contact Axon

question: I need IT help but I do not know what service I need.
plain_english_answer: That is common for business owners. The issue may involve website, email, hosting, domain, AI, automation, security, CRM, payment, cloud files or staff workflow. The best first step is to describe the business problem in plain English instead of trying to name the technology.
recommended_action: Share what is not working, what business result you want, your website or domain, screenshots and whether the issue affects sales, staff or customers.
when_to_call_axon: Ask Axon if the issue affects enquiries, email, payments, security, customer data, staff productivity or business operations.
follow_up_questions: Is the issue affecting sales, staff, customers or operations?
Do you already have a vendor or system?
cta_text: Ask Axon to guide the next step

question: Can Axon advise me before I spend money on software or AI?
plain_english_answer: Yes. Many companies buy tools before checking whether the tool fits the workflow, staff ability, data, budget and long-term support needs. A short advisory review can prevent wrong subscriptions, duplicated systems and poor adoption.
recommended_action: List the tools being considered, current pain points, number of users, budget, data involved and what result management expects.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon before buying software, AI tools, CRM, email systems, hosting, website platforms or automation tools that affect staff or customers.
follow_up_questions: How many staff will use it?
cta_text: Ask Axon to review the options

question: My website looks old. Should I repair it or rebuild it?
plain_english_answer: If the website is slow, hard to edit, not mobile friendly, or no longer explains your business clearly, a revamp is usually better than small repairs. If the design is still acceptable and only a few items are broken, repair may be enough.
recommended_action: Check the website design, mobile view, speed, content, contact forms and Google visibility before deciding.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if the site affects enquiries, credibility, SEO or business growth.
cta_text: Ask Axon to review the website

question: Why is my website not showing on Google?
plain_english_answer: A website may not show on Google because it is new, not indexed, has weak content, technical SEO issues, poor page titles, slow loading speed, or lacks business relevance signals.
recommended_action: Check Google Search Console, sitemap, robots.txt, page titles, content quality and whether Google has indexed the pages.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if the website is important for enquiries and Google traffic but does not appear for business keywords.
follow_up_questions: Is the site connected to Google Search Console?
What keywords do you expect to appear for?
cta_text: Ask Axon to check search visibility

question: My website contact form is not sending email.
plain_english_answer: This usually means the form, mail server, website plugin, SMTP settings, spam filter, DNS records or hosting mail routing is not working properly. The form may appear to submit, but the message does not reach the right mailbox.
recommended_action: Check whether the form stores submissions, whether SMTP is configured, whether the receiving mailbox works and whether SPF, DKIM and DMARC are correct.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if enquiries are important, messages are missing or the form affects sales and customer service.
follow_up_questions: Do messages reach spam or disappear completely?
cta_text: Ask Axon to check the form

question: Why is my website slow?
plain_english_answer: Websites are usually slow because of large images, heavy scripts, poor hosting, too many plugins, no caching, slow database queries or poor mobile optimization.
recommended_action: Check image sizes, hosting quality, caching, Core Web Vitals, mobile loading and unnecessary scripts.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if the site is affecting leads, Google ranking, mobile users or ad campaigns.
follow_up_questions: Is it slow on mobile, desktop or both?
Did this happen after a plugin or hosting change?
cta_text: Ask Axon to review speed

question: I made a website with AI. How do I publish it properly?
plain_english_answer: AI tools can create website drafts quickly, but publishing a real business website still needs domain setup, hosting, SSL, forms, SEO, tracking, security, mobile testing and backups.
recommended_action: Prepare the website files, domain name, hosting access, brand assets, contact form details and tracking requirements.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if you want the AI-generated website to become a proper live business website.
follow_up_questions: Do you have a domain and hosting?
Does it need forms, analytics or SEO?
cta_text: Ask Axon to publish it properly

question: My website does not look good on mobile.
plain_english_answer: Most visitors use mobile phones first. If the website is hard to read, buttons are too small, menus break, or forms are difficult to submit, it can reduce enquiries and trust.
recommended_action: Check the homepage, service pages, menu, form, WhatsApp button, images and loading speed on a real phone.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if mobile users are important for enquiries, local search, paid ads or customer communication.
follow_up_questions: What phone did you test on?
cta_text: Ask Axon to check mobile view

question: My website gets visitors but no enquiries.
plain_english_answer: Traffic alone is not enough. The site may not explain the offer clearly, may have weak calls-to-action, poor trust signals, slow pages, confusing navigation or forms that are not easy to use.
recommended_action: Review the landing pages, service wording, testimonials, contact options, WhatsApp button, form tracking and Google Analytics conversion data.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if the website is getting traffic but not generating business leads.
follow_up_questions: Do you have WhatsApp on the site?
cta_text: Ask Axon to improve enquiries

question: Who will maintain my website after it goes live?
plain_english_answer: A business website still needs updates after launch. Maintenance may include content updates, plugin and security updates, backups, forms, analytics, hosting checks and small layout fixes.
recommended_action: Decide how often the site needs updates, who approves content, and whether updates affect SEO or forms.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if you want reliable website maintenance and support instead of ad-hoc fixes.
follow_up_questions: Is the site WordPress or custom?
How often do you need changes?
cta_text: Ask Axon about maintenance

question: My website is down or showing a blank screen.
plain_english_answer: A website can go down because of hosting issues, expired domain or SSL, PHP errors, plugin conflicts, DNS changes, server overload, malware or broken files. A blank screen often needs log checking, not guessing.
recommended_action: Check whether the domain loads, hosting is active, SSL is valid, DNS is correct and whether the server error log shows a problem.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon urgently if customers cannot access the website, the site is blank, or business enquiries are affected.
follow_up_questions: Was there a plugin, DNS or hosting change?
cta_text: Ask Axon to check urgently

question: Is my homepage good enough for customers and AI search?
plain_english_answer: A good homepage should quickly explain who you help, what problem you solve, your services, trust signals, location or market, contact options and clear next steps. For AI search, the wording must also be easy for machines to understand.
recommended_action: Review the headline, service sections, FAQs, contact paths, schema, internal links and whether the page answers real customer questions.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if the homepage is important for credibility, search visibility, AI recommendations and enquiries.
cta_text: Ask Axon to review the homepage

question: Can ChatGPT or AI recommend my business?
plain_english_answer: AI tools can only recommend a business confidently when the website and public information clearly explain the services, location, expertise, trust signals and useful answers. Old-style SEO alone may not be enough.
recommended_action: Improve service pages, FAQs, structured data, clear business descriptions, helpful articles, location signals, contact details and AI-friendly content.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if you want the website to be clearer for Google, ChatGPT-style answers and business recommendations.
follow_up_questions: What services should AI recommend you for?
Do you have FAQ or service pages?
Is your business information consistent online?
cta_text: Ask Axon to improve AI search visibility

question: Do I still need SEO because of AI search?
plain_english_answer: Yes. SEO is still important, but it now needs to work together with AEO and GEO. Search engines and AI tools both need clear content, trusted signals, structured pages and answers to real customer questions.
recommended_action: Keep SEO basics strong while adding FAQ content, service clarity, schema, AI-friendly summaries, local signals and helpful business explanations.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if your website needs to be visible on Google and also understandable to AI tools.
follow_up_questions: Do you already get Google traffic?
Do you have helpful service pages?
cta_text: Ask Axon to plan SEO and AI visibility

question: Should I run Google Ads or fix my website first?
plain_english_answer: If the website is unclear, slow, not mobile friendly or has weak enquiry flow, paid ads may waste money. It is usually better to fix the landing page, tracking and contact path before increasing ad spend.
recommended_action: Check landing page clarity, speed, mobile view, form tracking, WhatsApp, conversion goals and keyword relevance before running ads.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if you plan to spend on ads and want the website to convert visitors into enquiries.
follow_up_questions: What is the monthly ad budget?
cta_text: Ask Axon to check ad readiness

question: Do I need Google Analytics or Search Console?
plain_english_answer: Yes, business websites should usually have both. Google Analytics helps understand visitors and conversions. Search Console helps understand indexing, search queries and technical visibility in Google.
recommended_action: Set up GA4, Search Console, sitemap submission, conversion events and basic reporting.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if you need clean tracking, reports or visibility checks before SEO, ads or website revamp work.
follow_up_questions: Do you have Search Console access?
cta_text: Ask Axon to check analytics

question: How can AI help my business if my staff are not technical?
plain_english_answer: AI can help non-technical staff draft replies, summarize documents, prepare proposals, answer common customer questions, organize information and reduce repetitive work. The key is to start with simple daily tasks, not complex systems.
recommended_action: List repetitive work done by staff every week, then identify which tasks can be assisted by AI safely.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if you want AI to be used properly across the company with business rules, privacy, staff guidance and practical workflows.
cta_text: Ask Axon to plan AI adoption

question: Do I need a chatbot or an AI assistant on my website?
plain_english_answer: A chatbot is useful when visitors often ask repeated questions. An AI assistant is better when you want visitors to explain their problem and receive guided recommendations. For business websites, the assistant should help qualify enquiries, not just answer basic FAQs.
recommended_action: Review your common customer questions, services, enquiry flow and whether the website already explains things clearly.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if you want the assistant to recommend services, guide visitors and connect to your business enquiry process.
follow_up_questions: What questions do customers ask most?
Should it connect to WhatsApp or a form?
cta_text: Ask Axon to design the chatbot flow

question: Can AI reply to customers for us?
plain_english_answer: AI can draft replies and answer common questions, but business rules are important. For customer-facing replies, it is safer to start with draft mode, approval steps, clear knowledge sources and escalation to a human when needed.
recommended_action: Prepare approved FAQs, service descriptions, pricing rules, escalation rules and examples of good replies.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if customer replies must follow company rules, protect privacy and connect to enquiry or CRM workflows.
follow_up_questions: Should replies be automatic or draft-only?
What topics must be escalated to a human?
cta_text: Ask Axon to plan a safe AI reply workflow

question: Should my company use ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini or a company AI workspace?
plain_english_answer: Public AI tools are useful for individuals, but companies often need shared knowledge, team rules, privacy, project history and business memory. The right choice depends on how staff collaborate and what information must stay within the company.
recommended_action: Review number of users, privacy needs, common workflows, document usage, API budget and whether company knowledge should be retained.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if you want AI to support company workflows rather than scattered individual chats.
follow_up_questions: Should chats be shared or private?
cta_text: Ask Axon to compare AI options

question: Why are my emails going to spam?
plain_english_answer: Emails often go to spam when SPF, DKIM or DMARC records are missing or wrong, the domain reputation is weak, the message looks promotional, or the mail server is not trusted.
recommended_action: Check domain DNS records, mail authentication, email content, sending reputation and whether the domain or server is blacklisted.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if staff emails are landing in spam, clients are not receiving emails, or email delivery affects business operations.
follow_up_questions: Are SPF, DKIM and DMARC set?
Is this for normal email or bulk sending?
cta_text: Ask Axon to check email delivery

question: My company email is not working.
plain_english_answer: Business email can fail because of DNS changes, expired domain, full mailbox, wrong MX records, password issues, mail server problems, Microsoft 365 or Google Workspace settings, or device configuration.
recommended_action: Check whether sending, receiving or both are affected, then verify domain expiry, MX records, mailbox quota, password, webmail and mail server status.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon urgently if staff cannot send or receive business-critical emails.
follow_up_questions: Can you send, receive or neither?
What email platform do you use?
Was DNS or hosting changed recently?
cta_text: Ask Axon to troubleshoot email

question: Outlook keeps asking for password.
plain_english_answer: Outlook password prompts can happen because of wrong password, expired password, MFA changes, old mail profiles, server settings, Microsoft 365 authentication issues, IMAP/SMTP mismatch or cached credentials.
recommended_action: Test webmail first, confirm the password, check MFA, remove old saved credentials and verify the mail account settings.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if several staff are affected or the issue started after migration, password changes or Microsoft 365 setup.
cta_text: Ask Axon to check Outlook

question: What are IMAP, SMTP and POP3?
plain_english_answer: IMAP is used to read and sync email across devices. SMTP is used to send email. POP3 downloads email to one device and is less suitable for modern business teams. For most companies, IMAP, Google Workspace or Microsoft 365 is better than POP3.
recommended_action: Confirm the correct incoming and outgoing server names, ports, SSL/TLS settings and whether the company should move to Google Workspace or Microsoft 365.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if multiple staff, old devices, migrations or domain DNS records are involved.
follow_up_questions: Which device or app are you setting up?
Is it cPanel, Google Workspace or Microsoft 365?
Do you need old emails migrated?
cta_text: Ask Axon to check mail settings

question: Should my company use Google Workspace or Microsoft 365?
plain_english_answer: Google Workspace is usually simpler for email, collaboration and shared documents. Microsoft 365 is usually better when the company depends heavily on Outlook, Excel, Word, Teams or Windows-based workflows.
recommended_action: Review how staff work today, what email system they prefer, file sharing needs, budget, security and migration complexity.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon before migration if there are many users, old emails, shared mailboxes, domains or business-critical email accounts.
follow_up_questions: Do staff prefer Outlook or Gmail?
How much old email must be migrated?
cta_text: Ask Axon to compare Google and Microsoft

question: Can we migrate from Google Workspace to Microsoft 365 or from Microsoft 365 to Google Workspace?
plain_english_answer: Yes. Email migration is possible in both directions, but it must be planned carefully to avoid downtime, missing email, DNS mistakes and staff confusion.
recommended_action: Prepare the user list, domain access, old mailbox sizes, shared mailboxes, aliases, DNS access, migration date and staff instructions.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if there are many users, old emails, critical accounts, shared mailboxes or a fixed cutover date.
follow_up_questions: Do you need old emails and contacts migrated?
cta_text: Ask Axon to plan the migration

question: A staff member left and we cannot access or control their email.
plain_english_answer: This is a common company control issue. The business should secure the account, reset access, preserve business emails, remove old devices, change recovery settings and ensure admin ownership is clear.
recommended_action: Confirm who controls the Google Workspace, Microsoft 365, cPanel or domain admin account, then secure the mailbox and recover business data where possible.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if the mailbox contains important client records, invoices, quotations or management communication.
follow_up_questions: Do you need to preserve old emails?
cta_text: Ask Axon to help secure access

question: My mailbox is full or old emails are missing.
plain_english_answer: Mailbox issues may be caused by storage limits, POP3 downloads, old Outlook data files, deleted items, retention settings, migration mistakes or server mailbox quotas.
recommended_action: Check mailbox quota, webmail, deleted items, archive files, Outlook data files and whether POP3 was used before.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if business records, client emails or migration history may be affected.
follow_up_questions: Are emails missing in webmail too?
cta_text: Ask Axon to review email storage

question: What hosting does my business need?
plain_english_answer: The right hosting depends on the website type, traffic, email needs, security, backups, speed, support and whether the site is WordPress, custom code, e-commerce or a business application.
recommended_action: Review website platform, storage, traffic, email setup, backup needs, SSL, security and support expectations.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if the website is business-critical or you want corporate hosting support instead of generic mass hosting.
follow_up_questions: Is the site WordPress or custom?
cta_text: Ask Axon to recommend hosting

question: What is cPanel and do I need it?
plain_english_answer: cPanel is a hosting control panel used to manage website files, email accounts, DNS, SSL, databases and backups. It is useful when you need practical hosting management without managing a server manually.
recommended_action: Check whether your website, email, databases and backups are hosted in cPanel, and who has admin access.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if you need cPanel hosting, migration, cleanup, email setup, SSL, backup or security help.
follow_up_questions: Is the issue website, email or DNS?
cta_text: Ask Axon to check cPanel

question: Do I need shared hosting, VPS or dedicated server?
plain_english_answer: Shared hosting is enough for simple websites. VPS or dedicated servers are better for heavier websites, business applications, higher control, better isolation, custom software or performance needs.
recommended_action: Review traffic, application type, database size, email requirements, security, backups and support needs.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if the server hosts business-critical websites, client systems, email or applications.
follow_up_questions: What application will run on it?
Who will manage updates and security?
cta_text: Ask Axon to review server needs

question: I do not know who controls my domain or DNS.
plain_english_answer: This is risky because domain and DNS control affects website, email, SSL and business identity. The company should know the registrar, admin email, DNS provider, nameservers and renewal status.
recommended_action: Find the registrar, domain login, admin email, nameservers, renewal date and whether a previous vendor controls access.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if a previous vendor controls the domain, DNS is unclear, email stopped after DNS changes or the domain is close to expiry.
cta_text: Ask Axon to check domain control

question: My website or email stopped after DNS changes.
plain_english_answer: DNS connects your domain to website and email services. A wrong A record, MX record, nameserver, SPF, DKIM or CNAME can break the website, email delivery or both.
recommended_action: Check nameservers, A records, MX records, SPF, DKIM, DMARC, CNAMEs, TTL and whether the old DNS records were copied correctly.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if both website and email are affected or if changes were made without a proper DNS record backup.
follow_up_questions: Is website, email or both affected?
cta_text: Ask Axon to fix DNS

question: My website says not secure or SSL expired.
plain_english_answer: The browser shows not secure when SSL is missing, expired, incorrectly installed or when the site loads insecure content. It can reduce trust and block enquiries.
recommended_action: Check the SSL certificate, expiry date, HTTPS redirect, mixed content and hosting SSL settings.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if customers see browser warnings or the SSL issue affects forms, payment or login pages.
follow_up_questions: Is the domain using Cloudflare or cPanel SSL?
cta_text: Ask Axon to fix SSL

question: How do I know if my website has been hacked?
plain_english_answer: Common warning signs include unknown pages, strange redirects, spam links, browser security warnings, sudden traffic drops, blocked emails, or Google showing unsafe website warnings.
recommended_action: Check website files, admin users, plugins, redirects, server logs, Google Search Console and malware scan results.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon urgently if customers see warnings, your site redirects elsewhere, or Google marks the website as unsafe.
follow_up_questions: Do you still have admin access?
cta_text: Ask Axon to check security

question: Do I need website backups and can deleted data be recovered?
plain_english_answer: Backups are important because websites can break after updates, hacking, server issues or human mistakes. Recovery depends on whether clean backups exist and how often they are taken.
recommended_action: Check hosting backups, offsite backups, database backups, backup frequency and whether restores are tested.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if the website, database, email or client records are business-critical and recovery must be reliable.
follow_up_questions: When was the last clean backup?
cta_text: Ask Axon to check backup readiness

question: How do I secure company accounts and remove old staff access?
plain_english_answer: Company security starts with account ownership, strong passwords, MFA, admin control, removing old staff, reviewing recovery emails and limiting access to files, email, hosting and domains.
recommended_action: Prepare a list of users, admins, shared accounts, devices, recovery emails, domains, hosting and cloud systems.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if staff turnover, shared passwords, admin confusion or sensitive client data is involved.
follow_up_questions: Do you know all admin accounts?
cta_text: Ask Axon to review access

question: Can I accept online payments on my website?
plain_english_answer: Yes. Businesses can accept online payments through payment gateways, payment links, e-commerce checkout, booking forms or invoice payment flows. The right method depends on the product, service, country, currency and approval process.
recommended_action: Clarify what you sell, currency, bank account, payment provider, refund process, invoice flow and whether payment must connect to forms or CRM.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if payments must connect to website forms, invoices, customer records or business workflows.
follow_up_questions: Do you need invoices or e-commerce checkout?
cta_text: Ask Axon to plan payment flow

question: Do I need a CRM to manage enquiries and follow-ups?
plain_english_answer: A CRM helps when leads come from website forms, WhatsApp, email, calls or social media and staff need to track follow-ups. Without a CRM, enquiries are easily lost or handled inconsistently.
recommended_action: Map where leads come from, who handles them, what follow-up stages exist and what reports management needs.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if missed follow-ups, poor visibility or scattered customer records are affecting sales.
follow_up_questions: What follow-up stages do you need?
cta_text: Ask Axon to design the CRM flow

question: Can WhatsApp enquiries go into a CRM?
plain_english_answer: Yes. WhatsApp enquiries can be organized into a CRM-style process with customer details, enquiry notes, follow-up status, reminders and staff assignment. The exact setup depends on whether you use normal WhatsApp, WhatsApp Business or API-based tools.
recommended_action: Decide how WhatsApp messages should be captured, who replies, what customer fields are needed and how follow-ups are tracked.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if WhatsApp is a major sales channel and enquiries are being missed or not tracked.
follow_up_questions: Do you need reminders or reports?
cta_text: Ask Axon to plan WhatsApp CRM

question: Can I automate quotations, invoices and customer follow-ups?
plain_english_answer: Yes. Many quotation, invoice, reminder and follow-up tasks can be automated using forms, email templates, CRM tools, payment links and workflow automation. The best solution depends on how your team currently handles enquiries and payments.
recommended_action: Map the current process from enquiry to quotation, approval, invoice, payment and follow-up.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if the process involves multiple staff, repeated manual work, missed follow-ups or customer delays.
cta_text: Ask Axon to map the workflow

question: Should I build a custom app or use ready-made software?
plain_english_answer: Ready-made software is usually faster and cheaper if it fits the process. A custom app is better when your workflow is unique, needs integration, or ready-made tools force staff to work the wrong way.
recommended_action: Compare your process, budget, users, data, reports, integration needs and long-term support before deciding.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon before building or buying if the system affects daily operations, customers or management reporting.
follow_up_questions: What reports or integrations are required?
cta_text: Ask Axon to compare options

question: I need an inventory system.
plain_english_answer: An inventory system should track products, stock movement, suppliers, sales, low-stock alerts and reports. The right approach depends on whether you sell online, in-store, by quotation or across multiple locations.
recommended_action: Map product types, stock locations, sales channels, purchase process, reports and whether the system must connect to POS, website or accounting.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if inventory affects sales, operations, purchasing or reporting across staff or branches.
follow_up_questions: Do you need barcode or POS integration?
cta_text: Ask Axon to review inventory workflow

question: I need a booking or appointment system.
plain_english_answer: A booking system helps customers choose services, dates, staff, locations and receive confirmations. It may also need deposits, reminders, cancellation rules and calendar integration.
recommended_action: Define services, duration, staff availability, locations, payment rules, reminder channels and admin reports.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if booking affects customer experience, payments, staff scheduling or operations.
follow_up_questions: Should it sync with Google Calendar or Outlook?
cta_text: Ask Axon to plan the booking flow

question: I need a staff attendance or HR system.
plain_english_answer: Staff attendance systems can track clock-in, leave, shifts, locations, approvals and reports. The right setup depends on company size, locations, payroll needs and whether staff work remotely or on-site.
recommended_action: Map staff count, locations, clock-in method, leave approvals, payroll needs and reporting requirements.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if attendance affects payroll, compliance, scheduling or multi-branch operations.
cta_text: Ask Axon to review attendance needs

question: I need a customer portal or member portal.
plain_english_answer: A portal is useful when customers, members or partners need to log in, view information, submit forms, download documents, pay invoices or track requests.
recommended_action: Define user roles, login rules, data shown, forms, documents, notifications, payments and admin controls.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if the portal handles customer data, payments, documents or business workflow approvals.
follow_up_questions: What should users see or submit?
cta_text: Ask Axon to plan the portal

question: Should we use Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox or SharePoint?
plain_english_answer: The right file platform depends on how staff collaborate, how permissions are managed, whether the company uses Google Workspace or Microsoft 365, and how sensitive the files are.
recommended_action: Review current file locations, staff access, sharing rules, folder structure, backup needs and whether files belong to departments or projects.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if files are scattered, permissions are unclear, staff cannot find documents or company data needs better control.
follow_up_questions: Which email platform do you use?
Are permissions by department or project?
cta_text: Ask Axon to organize cloud files

question: Can Axon support us remotely?
plain_english_answer: Many website, email, cloud, DNS, hosting and software issues can be reviewed remotely. Remote support is often faster when screenshots, login context and a clear issue summary are provided.
recommended_action: Prepare the issue description, affected users, screenshots, domain or website URL, and whether remote access is allowed.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon if the issue affects business operations or needs someone technical to review safely.
follow_up_questions: What device or system is affected?
cta_text: Ask Axon for remote support

question: How much does a website, chatbot, email migration or IT project cost?
plain_english_answer: Cost depends on scope, urgency, number of users, amount of content, integrations, design quality, security needs and support requirements. A simple fix may be small, but a proper business system or migration needs planning.
recommended_action: Share the website or domain, what you want to achieve, current problem, number of users and preferred timeline so Axon can advise the right scope.
when_to_call_axon: Call Axon when the decision affects sales, customer service, staff productivity, data, email or business operations.
follow_up_questions: How many users or pages are involved?
cta_text: Ask Axon for a practical estimate
